explanation: Adenosine is a naturally occurring nucleoside found in every living cell that activates skin repair signaling pathways and supports collagen and elastin synthesis, helping to firm skin and reduce the appearance of fine lines over time. It is non-irritating and well-tolerated by all skin types including reactive and sensitive.

Alanine is a non-essential amino acid that functions as a natural moisturizing factor (NMF) component in the skin, helping to attract and retain water in the upper skin layers. It is biocompatible and well-tolerated, contributing to hydration and barrier integrity alongside other NMF components like sodium PCA and urea.

Alaria esculenta extract, derived from Atlantic wakame (a brown seaweed), is rich in fucoidans, alginates, and polyphenols that provide antioxidant protection and help defend skin against environmental stressors. It is well-tolerated and is used in anti-aging and protective formulas for its ability to neutralize free radicals and support skin firmness.

Alchemilla vulgaris (lady's mantle) extract is an astringent botanical antioxidant rich in tannins and flavonoids, historically used to calm skin irritation and tighten pores. It is well-tolerated at typical formula concentrations and is used in soothing and pore-refining products for its anti-inflammatory and free-radical-scavenging properties.

Alcohol (ethyl alcohol) is used in skincare as a solvent and penetration enhancer that helps actives absorb more quickly into skin. At high concentrations it can disrupt the skin barrier and cause dryness on sensitive or reactive skin, but in small amounts as a solubilizer it is generally well-tolerated.

Alcohol denat is ethyl alcohol that has been denatured (made undrinkable) with additives, and is used in skincare as a solvent, texture-thinning agent, and penetration enhancer. It evaporates quickly and creates a stripped, tight feeling on reactive or barrier-compromised skin, disrupting the lipid matrix and accelerating transepidermal water loss with regular use.

Alcohol denat. is ethyl alcohol denatured with additives, used in skincare as a fast-drying solvent and penetration enhancer that thins formula texture and helps actives absorb quickly. It creates a tight, stripped sensation on reactive or sensitized skin and with regular use degrades the lipid barrier, increasing transepidermal water loss.

Aleurites moluccana (kukui nut) seed oil is a lightweight plant oil from Hawaii rich in linoleic and alpha-linolenic fatty acids that absorb readily into skin and support barrier repair. It is particularly beneficial for dry, sensitized, or sun-damaged skin and has a relatively low comedogenic rating, making it suitable for combination skin types.

Algae extract is a broad term for extracts from various seaweed species, typically providing firming, antioxidant, and hydration benefits through their polysaccharide and mineral content. Tolerance varies by species — most are well-tolerated, though some algae derivatives have moderate comedogenic potential on oily or acne-prone skin.

Algae extract is a broad term for extracts from various seaweed species, typically providing firming, antioxidant, and hydration benefits through their polysaccharide and mineral content. Tolerance varies by species — most are well-tolerated, though some algae derivatives have moderate comedogenic potential on oily or acne-prone skin.

Algin (sodium alginate) is a natural polysaccharide extracted from brown seaweed, used as a thickener and gelling agent that gives creams and masks their texture. It is well-tolerated, non-comedogenic, and can hold large amounts of water, making it a gentle hydrating matrix component suitable for all skin types.

Allantoin is a soothing active derived from comfrey root that promotes cell turnover and calms skin irritation by accelerating the shedding of damaged surface cells. It is exceptionally well-tolerated and is widely used in formulas for sensitive, reactive, and post-procedure skin to reduce redness and support healing.

Aloe is a common shorthand for aloe vera gel or extract from the Aloe barbadensis plant, a soothing botanical with anti-inflammatory, hydrating, and wound-healing properties. It is generally well-tolerated by sensitive skin in well-formulated products, though concentrated raw gel can form a film as it dries that may cause tightness or an itch-and-scratch cycle on reactive skin.

Aloe barbadensis flower extract is a soothing botanical derived from the flower of the aloe plant, containing polyphenols and glycoproteins with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It is well-tolerated and is used in calming formulas to reduce redness and support skin comfort.

Aloe barbadensis leaf extract is a concentrated form of aloe vera containing polysaccharides, enzymes, and anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe irritation and support wound healing. It is generally well-tolerated and particularly beneficial for reactive, sunburned, or post-procedure skin.

Aloe barbadensis leaf juice powder is spray-dried or freeze-dried aloe vera juice used to deliver aloe's soothing and anti-inflammatory benefits in anhydrous or powdered formulas. It is well-tolerated and reconstitutes in water to provide the same calming acemannan polysaccharides found in fresh aloe gel.

Aloe barbadensis leaf water is the water-phase fraction of aloe vera, providing a lighter delivery of aloe's soothing polysaccharides and anti-inflammatory compounds without the thicker gel matrix. It is well-tolerated, non-comedogenic, and used as a gentle hydrating base in toners, mists, and lightweight lotions.

Aloe vera is the widely recognized soothing botanical from the Aloe barbadensis plant, containing acemannan polysaccharides, vitamins, and enzymes that calm inflammation, support wound healing, and deliver lightweight hydration. It is well-tolerated by most skin types, though concentrated gel forms can create a tightening film as they dry that may irritate very reactive or sensitive skin.

Alpha-lipoic acid is a potent fat- and water-soluble antioxidant that neutralizes free radicals in both the lipid and aqueous layers of skin cells, making it unusually effective at preventing oxidative damage. It can cause tingling or mild irritation on sensitive or reactive skin, particularly at higher concentrations, and is best used in well-buffered formulas.

Alpha-isomethyl ionone is a synthetic violet-like fragrance compound classified as a known contact allergen by the EU Scientific Committee on Consumer Safety (SCCS). It is one of 26 fragrance allergens that must be disclosed by name on EU cosmetic labels, and can trigger allergic contact dermatitis and an itch-and-scratch cycle on reactive or fragrance-sensitive skin.

Althaea rosea (hollyhock) flower extract is a mucilaginous botanical rich in polysaccharides and flavonoids with soothing and film-forming properties that calm irritated skin and help retain surface moisture. It is well-tolerated and used in calming and hydrating formulas as a gentle skin-conditioning agent.

Alumina (aluminum oxide) is a mineral compound used in skincare as a thickener, abrasive in physical exfoliants, and opacity agent in mineral makeup. The fine particles used in cosmetics are considered inert and well-tolerated, though sharp-edged alumina abrasives in scrubs can cause microtears on reactive or sensitive skin.

Aluminum chlorohydrate is an antiperspirant active that temporarily blocks sweat ducts in the underarm area to reduce perspiration. It is well-established in antiperspirant safety data and is considered well-tolerated for underarm use, though it can cause mild irritation on freshly shaved or broken skin.

Aluminum dimyristate is an aluminum salt of myristic acid used as a thickener and consistency agent in stick and anhydrous formulas such as deodorants and lip products. It is considered well-tolerated at typical use concentrations and contributes a smooth, waxy texture without significant sensitization risk.

Aluminum hydroxide is a mild inorganic compound used in skincare as a pH buffer and neutralizing agent, and in sunscreens as a coating for titanium dioxide particles to improve dispersibility. It is well-tolerated and inert at typical cosmetic use levels.

Aluminum hydroxide is a mild inorganic compound used in skincare as a pH buffer and neutralizing agent, and in sunscreens as a coating for titanium dioxide particles to improve dispersibility. It is well-tolerated and inert at typical cosmetic use levels.

Aluminum starch octenylsuccinate is a modified starch that functions as an absorbent, anti-caking agent, and skin-feel enhancer in powders and anhydrous formulas, helping to control oil and impart a silky texture. It is considered well-tolerated and is commonly used in dry shampoos, setting powders, and lightweight moisturizers.

Aluminum starch octenylsuccinate is a modified starch that functions as an absorbent, anti-caking agent, and skin-feel enhancer in powders and anhydrous formulas, helping to control oil and impart a silky texture. It is considered well-tolerated and is commonly used in dry shampoos, setting powders, and lightweight moisturizers.

Amber powder is finely milled fossilized tree resin used in skincare as a texture agent, mild exfoliant, and aesthetic ingredient believed to have antioxidant trace mineral content. It is generally well-tolerated, though very fine mineral powders can occasionally cause irritation on highly reactive or compromised skin.

Aminomethyl propanol (AMP) is an organic amine used in skincare primarily as a pH adjuster and neutralizer for carbomer and acrylate polymer thickeners, which require a base to activate their gelling function. It is considered well-tolerated at typical use concentrations and is non-sensitizing in finished formulas.
